explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **text segments that introduce or describe a notable individual** (such as a named person with their profession, title, achievement, or biographical detail), particularly when the person is being framed in a professional, artistic, or public role.

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across multiple languages (English, Italian, Spanish, Romanian, Slovak, etc.) and domains including news articles, biographies, and cultural reporting. It activates on mentions of celebrities, artists, politicians, activists, athletes, professionals, and other public figures, often at the point where their name is coupled with a defining role or accomplishment (e.g., "Grammy-winning saxophonist Ravi Coltrane," "Progressive activist Ady Barkan," "Arsenal legend Paul Merson," "Ghanaian actor and politician John Dumelo").

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The low-activation texts lack a focused introduction or description of a specific, notable individual. Instead, they discuss general events, abstract concepts, impersonal news, or processes (e.g., stock market movements, earthquake reports, policy statements, horoscopes, or system descriptions). The high-activation texts consistently center on a person’s identity or actions, while the low-activation texts are either impersonal or mention individuals only peripherally.

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects semantic contexts where a person is being profiled or highlighted as a subject of interest, acting as a trigger for biographical or role-based information. It essentially fires on textual cues that signal "this is about a specific person and their notable attributes."

results.actual_text: Der Bundestrainer im Porträt Alfred Gislason: Die Mission des pünktlichen Isländers Die Erwartungen
results.plain_text: Der Bundestrainer im Porträt Alfred Gislason: Die Mission des pünktlichen Isländers  Die Erwartungen an Alfred Gislason sind groß. Der Bundestrainer soll die Handball-Nationalmannschaft bei der EM in Deutschland zu einer Medaille coachen. Mit Druck weiß der 64-Jährige umzugehen - ein persönlicher Schicksalsschlag lässt ihn anders aufs Leben blicken.  Rückblickend war das eine ziemlich gute Idee, die Adelheidur Alfredsdottir und Gisli Hjartarson damals rund um den 7. September 1959 hatten. Alfred nannten sie ihren zweitgeborenen Sohn – der Name setzt sich aus den Begriffen "Naturgeist" und "Ratgeber" zusammen. Im weiteren Verlauf der Jahrzehnte sollte sich zeigen, dass dieser früh nach Erfolg strebende Alfred Gislason aus Akureyri im Norden Islands, diesen Namen förmlich mit Leben ausfüllte.  Mutter fördert früh selbstständiges Handeln  Fünf Geschwister saßen in Kindertagen neben ihm am Tisch, darunter vier Brüder. Da war von Anfang an Durchsetzungswille gefragt. "Sie waren immer hungrig, unsere Kinder. Wenn man zu spät zum Essen kam, war nichts mehr da", sagt Alfredsdottir. Der ehemaligen Bankangestellten lag es am Herzen, dass ihre Kinder sich früh eigene Gedanken machten. "Ich habe nie gesagt, seid ruhig und macht das so, wie ich es will. Mir war wichtig, dass sie ihr Bestes geben und selbstständig handeln. Und das von Beginn an."  Alfred Gislason (r.) mit seinen Eltern in Akureyri auf Island.  Ihre Söhne schafften es allesamt – in verschiedenen Sportarten – in isländische Nationalmannschaften. "Ich glaube, wir haben damit den isländischen Rekord", sagt Alfred Gislason mit einem Schmunzeln, als er Ende September seine Familie in Akureyri besucht, mit heute 20.000 Einwohnern die viertgrößte Stadt des Inselstaates.  Nach Krebstod seiner Frau – Gislason findet zweite große Liebe  Gislason ließ sich beim Trip in seine Heimat von der Sportschau begleiten, die von Reporter Hendrik Deichmann produzierte Dokumentation "Alfred Gislason - Schicksalstage des Bundestrainers" ist in der ARD Mediathek und am Sonntag, 7. Januar, um 16.50 Uhr in der ARD zu sehen.  Auf Island begleitet Gislason seine Lebensgefährtin Hrund Gunnsteinsdottir, die er ein Jahr nach dem Krebstod seiner Frau Kara kennenlernte, und durch die "wieder Fröhlichkeit in sein Leben zurückgekehrt ist", wie sein älterer Bruder Hjörtur es beschreibt.  "Alfred" – der Ratgeber mit der naturgegebenen Kraft  Dem imponiert an Alfred besonders dessen Hartnäckigkeit: "Er ist sehr stur. Wenn er er ein Ziel hat, will er es unbedingt erreichen." Gunnsteinsdottir spricht bei ihrem Alfred von einem "großartigen Charakter, er ist explosiv in vielen Dingen". Und er habe ein ganz besondere Gabe, sagt die Buchautorin.  "Er kann gut Menschen und ihre Charaktere lesen, und er ist wirklich interessiert an ihnen. Ich könnte mir vorstellen, dass dies angeboren ist. Das ist nicht etwas, das man unbedingt lernt. Das ist eine Art Instinkt", so Gunnsteinsdottir. "Er kümmert sich sehr darum, wie sich die Spieler fühlen. Er will für sie da sein, um das Beste aus ihnen herauszuholen, und das macht ihn zu einem wirklich sehr guten Coach."  Gislason mit DHB-Auswahl noch ohne großen Erfolg  Dass der ehemalige, kraftvolle Rückraumspieler von TuSEM Essen in die Kategorie "sehr guter Coach" fällt, weist auch seine Vita aus. Er ist der einzige Trainer, der mit zwei Vereinen (SC Magdeburg 2002, THW Kiel 2010 und 2012) die Champions League gewann. Hinzu kommen unter anderem noch sieben deutsche Meisterschaften und sechs Pokal-Triumphe. Nur mit der DHB-Auswahl gab es bislang noch nicht viel zu feiern. Seit knapp vier Jahren ist er Bundestrainer, der fünfte Platz bei der WM 2023 ist bislang die beste Platzierung.  Alfred Gislason beim Gewinn der Champions League mit dem THW Kiel 2012.  Bei der EM 2024, bei der Gastgeber Deutschland in der Vorrunde auf die Schweiz (10. Januar, 20.45 Uhr), Nordmazedonien (14. Januar, 20.30 Uhr) und Frankreich (16. Januar, 20.30 Uhr, live in der ARD und im Stream auf sportschau.de) trifft, wird mehr erwartet vom Isländer, der von 1983 an die meiste Zeit in Deutschland gelebt hat.  Das halbe Leben hier sei nicht ohne Folgen geblieben. "Ich bin ziemlich deutsch geworden, das sagen meine Freunde. Ich bin ungewöhnlich pünktlich für einen Isländer", sagt Gislason und lacht. "Ich denke schon, dass die Isländer sehr flexibel sind, aber das Planen nicht immer ihre Stärke ist. Sie sind irgendwie immer noch Jäger und Sammler."   Ich bin ungewöhnlich pünktlich für einen Isländer.  Nationalspieler, die schon vor vielen Jahren unter Gislason im Verein trainiert haben, sehen weitere Veränderungen an ihm. Wie etwa Rune Dahmke vom THW Kiel. "Ich finde, dass er in den letzten Jahren viel, viel lockerer geworden ist", so der Linksaußen. "Und ich glaube, dass er als Bundestrainer noch mal einen anderen Fokus darauf legt, dass gute Stimmung ist. Es ist ja immer nur ein kurzer Abschnitt. Da ist es extrem wichtig, dass das Teamgefüge passt."  Gislason räumt offen ein, dass er früher als Clubtrainer Fehler machte. "Ich war ein ziemlicher Diktator. Ich ging zu weit mit meiner Kritik oder wie ich mit Leuten umgegangen bin", sagt der 64-Jährige, dem aber auch wichtig ist: "Ich habe mir aber auch immer gesagt: Ich werde es nie so machen wie ein paar Trainer von mir, die auf eine Frage von mir geantwortet haben: 'Halt den Mund und mach, was ich sage.'" Er hat dieses Prinzip von seiner Mutter übernommen.   Ich war ein ziemlicher Diktator.  Die Einsicht, dass er im Umgang mit seinen Spielern oftmals zu schroff gewesen war, ist Gislason offenbar schon vor fünf, sechs Jahren gekommen. Dahmke attestiert ihm für "die letzte Zeit als Vereinstrainer eine viel lockerere Art". Auch das Einsammeln von Titeln, das Erreichen von Zielen, dürfte mit zunehmendem Alter zu einer entspannteren Haltung geführt haben.  Karas Tod lässt Gislason anders aufs Leben blicken  Am 31. Mai 2021 verschoben sich auf brutale Weise alle Relationen, was wichtig ist im Leben. Seine Frau Kara erlag ihrem Krebsleiden. Gislason war fortan allein auf dem restaurierten Resthof in der 50-Seelen-Gemeinde Wendgräben bei Magdeburg. "Wir wollten reisen, hier viel machen und das Leben genießen. Wir hatten die Hoffnung, dass wir es nach hinten verzögern könnten, auch wenn wir wussten, dass es nicht heilbar ist", sagt er.  Gislason fiel es enorm schwer, aber er setzte seine Mission als Bundestrainer fort. Und nun im Januar soll sie zu einem Erfolg führen. Ein Medaillenrang wäre ein solcher, der Titelgewinn natürlich das Wahrwerden eines Traumes. "Gerade so ein Turnier zu Hause in Deutschland – da baut sich schon eine Anspannung auf. Aber das ist mehr so eine positive Anspannung, eine Vorfreude auf das, was kommt. Und wenn es gut läuft, ist ganz Deutschland im Handballfieber", so der Bundestrainer.  Gislason will mit dem DHB-Team bei der Heim-EM 2024 ins Halbfinale.  Und nach dem Spektakel? Irgendwann auch wieder mit Lebensgefährtin Hrund nach Island, zu den eigenen Eltern, natürlich an Karas Grab. In den Norden der Vulkaninsel mit seinen manchmal etwas eigenwilligen Menschen, nach Akureyri, das von einer rauen Schale, einer schroffen Landschaft, umschlossen ist, das im Kern aber viel Wärme ausstrahlt – das Rotsignal an den Ampeln leuchtet in Herzform.

results.actual_text: . A young man from Bihar’s Samastipur, Amarjeet Jaikar, recently became an overnight star after his video went viral on many social media platforms
results.plain_text: New Delhi: In today’s world, social media works like a magic wand, especially for those who are talented and lack the means to access a platform to showcase their talent. A young man from Bihar’s Samastipur, Amarjeet Jaikar, recently became an overnight star after his video went viral on many social media platforms.  Amarjeet recently uploaded a video of himself singing a soulful rendition of the famous song ‘Dil De Diya Hai’ and it has won many hearts on the internet, including some Bollywood celebrities. Even Sonu Sood shared his video. Actress Nitu Chandra shared the video on Twitter and even enquired about his whereabouts. She wrote, “Who is this guy? Fabulous. Please send his contact no. Thanks”  Amarjeet frequently posts videos of himself singing along to different Bollywood songs. He hails from Shahpur Patori Bhabhua village, which is about 35 kilometres from Samastipur.  Speaking to ANI, Amarjeet expressed gratitude after his video got viral. He also revealed that Neetu Chandra even called him and promised to call him to Mumbai. He said, “Neetu Chandra mam ka call bhi aaya tha, unhone bola hai ki Mumbai bulaungi!”  When asked about his family background, he revealed that he is currently pursuing his studies and does stage shows in his village. His father has a small salon.  His mother further expressed how proud she was to hear about his son’s fame on social media. She revealed that many called him mad because he would sing so much, and wanted to pursue it however, now all of them are quite happy with him becoming an overnight star! She also revealed that Amarjeet used to work with caterers for weddings earlier, however, after some time, he started doing stage shows at weddings to earn some money.  Well, clearly, he has gained mass popularity over the past few days, thanks to social media! Hopefully, he will continue to shine and get some work due to it in the future.

results.actual_text: eski Zaman gazetesi genel yayın yönetmeni Ekrem Dumanlı ile örgütün üst düzey çok sayıda yöneticisi bulunuyor.
results.plain_text: İstanbul Anadolu Cumhuriyet Başsavcılığı'nın, Fetullahçı Terör Örgütü/Paralel Devlet Yapılanmasına (FETÖ/PDY) yönelik soruşturması kapsamında hakkında gözaltı kararı verilen 111 şüphelinin yakalanması için İstanbul merkezli 17 ilde operasyon başlatıldı.  Hakkında gözaltı kararı verilen 111 şüphelinin yakalanmasına yönelik operasyon kapsamında 39 kişi gözaltına alındı. Hakkında yakalama kararı bulunan bir kişinin cezaevinde olduğu ifade edildi.  Bu arada, gözaltı kararı verilenler arasında FETÖ/PDY elebaşı Fetullah Gülen, eski Zaman gazetesi genel yayın yönetmeni Ekrem Dumanlı ile örgütün üst düzey çok sayıda yöneticisi bulunuyor.  Gözaltı kararı verilenlerden 35'inin örgütün şifreli haberleşme programı ByLock kullanıcısı olduğu, 56 kişinin Bank Asya hesap hareketlerinde örgüt elebaşının çağrısı doğrultusunda hesap arttırımı bulunduğu, listedeki 10 kişinin örgütün çatı dosyasında adının geçtiği, 46 kişinin ise yurt dışında olduğuna dair bilgiler bulunduğu belirtildi.  Operasyon doğrultusunda yakalama ve gözaltı işlemleri devam ediyor.  Kaynak: AA

results.actual_text: The BREXIT paradox is now upon sub-Saharan Africa. That is, as it turns out, what is politically the right move for
results.plain_text: The BREXIT paradox is now upon sub-Saharan Africa. That is, as it turns out, what is politically the right move for the UK may turn out to be economic suicide. Or what may be the economic move for prosperity may turn out to be a diplomatic nightmare.  Already, fears of increased market volatility are apparent as evidenced by the pound sterling depreciation by amazing percentage points. As at the 24th June, the morning of the ground breaking outcome of the vote, the pound sterling was down 12percent on the USD and against the Euro it was down 9percent . This surely will result in a major shift in the trade decisions by prospective and incumbent investors in the days that will follow.  In this position note, we analyse the possible outcomes as a result of the referendum. The list is far from exhaustive as there are multiple diverging and sometimes parallel outcomes that can come as a result. But one thing remains absolutely certain: it will be a long, physically, politically and economically draining process for the pull-out to be finalised. The vote is just a stepping stone: what lies ahead for the UK, especially after David Cameron courageously threw in the towel, will be a long hard road. Volume of trade About half of the UK trade is with the EU. It leaves more questions than answers when a situation of this magnitude has taken this course. Trade statistics suggest that the three months ending April 2016 saw an increase in the deficit on trade in goods by 0.6billion. That is, imports increased by 2.7billion and exports by 2.1 billion bringing this to a record 24billion deficit in the three months. This is an indication of the confidence with which the business community has been endowed as a result of the BREXIT.  As at the G8 summit in Gleneagles, Scotland it was unanimously agreed that aid to Africa will be doubled and outstanding debts to some of the poorest countries will be eliminated. It was agreed that aid will be increased by at least 50 million with poor countries in Africa getting the lion’s share of the deal as half was to go towards Africa. HIV and AIDS, education and other public goods being a priority were to have increased investment from the UK. Now, the African child can only wonder. With the trade deficit widening, power shifting in the EU and economic might of the UK diminished, will this become a reality. But then again, there is very little to no influence on opening external trade for the UK by the EU. Therefore, some experts believe that a BREXIT will have no effect on the volume of trade by the UK.  On so many levels, a lot hinges on the type of cooperation that the UK will follow after this. The most likely for the sake of trade will be the Norwegian style EEA agreement in which “the UK joins the European Economic Area and maintains full access to the single market, but must adopt EU standards and regulations with little influence over these. The UK still makes a substantial contribution to the EU budget and is unable to impose immigration restrictions.” trade costs Indications are that trade with the UK and the EU will be a spectacle for economic spectators and pirates. If there was ever a “right time” to buy the pound, it must be after the shift in its weight following the BREXIT. It is evident that the deficit will widen further as UK goods, will become cheaper for the UK and expensive for the UK trade with the EU. This again will have considerably unfavourable effects on capital structures. There is bound to be some form of capital flight as investors will receive a blow on their wealth as the pound sterling plunges.  lower net contribution to the EU budget would reduce prises for Botswana The European development fund (EDF), which is now doing its 11th leg (2014-2020), was budgeted at a staggering 30.32 billion euros which represented an increase from the 10th leg by 13percent . Now, this is essentially 30percent  development aid of the EU. For those with mouths drooling at the time, sooner or later the reality is that what was promised may not be delivered. The UK contributes about 14percent of the EDF and just by rough calculation, this means a drop in the fund by 4.2billion euros, which far outweighs the increase that was achieved at 3.39 billion euros. This implies some change in what the poorest African countries were bound to receive. This again depends on the model of cooperation that will be adopted post the BREXIT. Fall in incomes-Reduced trade will have dampening effects on productivity, resulting in reduced incomes and ultimately hurting economic growth. Immigrants have played a major role in the growth of the UK economy but now, the future is uncertain. With the use of the Solow growth model that is hinged on endogenous growth it has been proved on different studies that human capital spill-overs have a positive impact on regional productivity and growth. And, FDI as associated with positive technology and human capital spill-over from one country to the other. Therefore, in this regard, it will be reduced.  Some experts though are of a different view. Immigration problems and loopholes have been a thorn on the UK. An increased diminishing of employment prospects for a Briton was a concern because of immigrants. For the tax payer, it brought negative effects as the public good definitely has to be financed in order for immigrants to enjoy equal social amenities. That meant a decrease in disposable incomes and welfare for a Briton. But now, with the BREXIT, all these may be reversed. This means the EU has little to no influence on immigration policies for the UK hence they will most likely tighten the grip on their borders. This will result in a low subsidy by a BRITON on the public good hence increasing disposable incomes.  reduction in bargaining power Now that the UK populace has voted out of the EU, bargaining power on the European front has been reduced. Though bilateral relations between Africa and UK will be strengthened, bilateral trade will be diminished largely due to reduced bargaining power. The process for the UK to re-negotiate relations will be a lengthy and economically taxing process. For African exports to the EU, this will have rough effects on their performance. Botswana’s beef exports to the EU are now facing an uncertain future. The renegotiation of prices and entry into the wider continental market of the EU leaves the spectator confused. On the one hand, the BMC, which has been struggling, will not be impressed by the outcome of the vote because of the cost of uncertainty. On the other hand, this may open a few doors which were closed by the inclusion of the UK in the EU. That is, the foot and mouth red tape that has been a thorn on the BMC beef export may be relaxed as bilateral relations improve. The EU market’s importance on the UK cannot be understated. What is evident though is that given its importance to the UK, and if Norway is anything to go by, they will be forced into the same single market rules. Sadly, being outside the EU will hurt the UK influence and hence a reduction in bargaining power.  In conclusion, most of the effects of a BREXIT are now more speculative than definite. There is a need for quantitative trade models to be used to forecast the potential damage or benefit to economic growth for Botswana and Africa as a whole.

predictions.text: But one thing remains absolutely certain: it will be a long, physically, politically and economically draining process for the pull-out to be finalised. The vote is just a stepping stone: what lies ahead for the UK, especially after David Cameron courageously threw in the towel, will be a long hard road.
